mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value at decision time was 455644.0
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return spreads versus lower growth in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value at decision time was 4.7
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if the triggered mean return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration while the federal funds rate remains low signals a future tightening risk that raises discount-rate uncertainty and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month growth exceeds its 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value at decision time was 0.37
regime_hint: early monetary tightening from near-zero rates
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration combined with FEDFUNDS below 1.0 is not followed by negative 60-day excess returns or if interaction terms add no predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum indicates improving factory output, capacity utilization, and cyclical earnings breadth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value at decision time was 103.5527
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO momentum fails to outperform negative momentum over 60 trading days in walk-forward tests or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in housing starts signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future pressure on construction, durable goods, and bank credit activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value at decision time was 1172.0
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside crossings are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered returns are not below untriggered returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals broad labor-income expansion without immediate labor-market deterioration, supporting consumption and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value at decision time was 143894.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-declining condition has lower 20-day returns than either condition alone, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A short-run increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening surprise that raises discount rates and reduces risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value at decision time was 0.37
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS increases of at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real activity combined with rising consumer prices signals stagflationary pressure that lowers real earnings growth while lifting discount-rate risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth falls below 1.0 while CPIAUCSL 3-month growth exceeds its 12-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value at decision time was 16505.1
failure_condition: Reject if weak GDPC1 growth with accelerating CPIAUCSL does not produce lower 60-day returns than normal-growth observations, or if the interaction term is insignificant at p > 0.10.
